name: parsel (Web Scraping HTML/XML Selector Library from Scrapy)

performance.notes: lxml-backed; fast XPath and CSS selectors

agent_readiness.notes: HTML/XML selector library extracted from Scrapy. from parsel import Selector. sel = Selector(text=html). sel.css('div.title::text').get(). sel.css('a::attr(href)').getall(). sel.xpath('//div[@class="main"]//h2/text()').get(). Chaining: sel.css('div').xpath('.//a').css('::text'). re(): sel.css('p::text').re(r'\d+'). Selector.from_response() for HTTP responses. Pseudo-elements: ::text, ::attr(name). SelectorList.get() / .getall() / .re(). Both XPath and CSS selectors — combines best of both worlds.

gotchas: ::text and ::attr() pseudo-elements are parsel-specific, not standard CSS
Scrapy ecosystem knowledge assumed in docs
No JavaScript rendering (pair with Playwright for JS-heavy sites)
best_when: Web scraping with both XPath and CSS selector flexibility
When Scrapy spider selectors are used standalone
Complex HTML extraction requiring chained selectors
Both XPath and CSS access from the same Selector
summary: parsel is the HTML/XML selector library extracted from Scrapy. Selector(html).css('div::text').get(). xpath('//a/@href').getall(). .re(r'pattern'). Chains CSS+XPath. ::text and ::attr() pseudo-elements. lxml-backed for speed. BSD-3. Best for scraping needing both XPath and CSS selectors. Pairs well with httpx for requests. For speed-critical CSS-only parsing use selectolax.
